title: The Forgotten Museum
setting: A quiet natural history archive corridor after hours. Polished parquet floors and glass display cases.
narrative_introduction: The lens opens into a hushed museum corridor. Marble busts and fossil display cases sleeping under soft security lighting. Look beyond the exhibits to what history left behind.

mechanics.observation: Locked-off 2-second cinematic moment. An elderly night guard walks past a glass display case, resting his palm on the wooden frame for exactly one second before checking his pocket watch.
mechanics.memory: Spatial fragment placement. Reconstruct the display corridor: polished marble bust, brass pocket watch, wooden case frame, hand imprint on glass, old museum ticket on floor, security lamp glow.
mechanics.discovery: Frozen moment exploration. Attuning to the wooden case and pocket watch reveals an inscription connecting the guard to the exhibition artifact.
mechanics.evidence: Archive entry reveals that the guard's grandfather originally donated the fossil inside the case eighty years ago.
mechanics.reflection: Iris voice synthesizes the connection between observer and history. Archive entry: 'The Forgotten Museum'.

environment.location: natural history archive corridor, glass display case

lighting.fill: soft amber glow from inside the wooden display case
lighting.key_detail: hand imprint visible on the glass where the guard rests his palm every night

camera.depth_of_field: deep, polished parquet floor reflections sharp
camera.color_grade: cool museum blues contrasted with rich mahogany amber

animation.guard: steps into frame (0.5s) -> rests palm on display frame (1.0s) -> checks watch (0.5s) = 2.0s
animation.dust_mote: fine archival dust drifting above the spotlight
animation.reflections: light sweeps across parquet floor with each step

ambient_details: mahogany glass display case containing an ammonite fossil
brass pocket watch with engraved lid
marble bust of 19th-century naturalist in background
faded 1942 exhibition stub tucked under the case pedestal

audio.base_layer: deep museum room acoustics, distant clock tick, faint HVAC drone
audio.focus_layer: leather boot squeak on parquet, brass watch click, palm touching polished wood

subtle: hand imprint on glass shows daily habit
watch engraving matches plaque on display case
deep: ammonite fossil plaque shares the guard's surname
intentionally_missed_first_view: brass watch lid contains a miniature photograph of the same fossil

reconstruction.iris_prompt: Reconstruct the corridor. Place the artifacts that carry history.

attunements.reveals: engraved lid reads: 'To Thomas Holloway, who unearthed the stone from the clay, 1942'
bronze plaque reads: 'Ammonite Specimen, Donated by the Holloway Family'
residual warmth shows exact alignment with decades of previous touch marks

investigation.iris_intervention: You have witnessed the unspoken bond. He does not guard stone; he guards his family's memory.

revelation.iris_response: You noticed the palm imprint on the glass. You noticed the engraved pocket watch. You noticed the Holloway family name shared between guard and artifact. History is not dead stone under glass; it lives through those who remember.

archive_mapping.title: The Forgotten Museum
archive_mapping.iris_note: Every night, the guard touched the mahogany frame where his grandfather's name was etched.
